channel.title: Microsoft Cloud Platform Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily updates from Azure and Microsoft 365 blogs

item.title: Istio‑Based Weighted Traffic Management on AKS for Enterprise Workloads
item.description: Istio‑Based Weighted Traffic Management Pre-Requisites: AKS containers (internal pods, no Envoy sidecar) Istio Installed and Configured at AKS Cluster (Ingress Gateway–based traffic management) Section 1 — Overview & Why Istio Is Required In modern distributed systems, traffic control is no longer...
item.dc:creator: Amreen

item.title: Welcome to Azure Cosmos DB Conf 2026
item.description: Today is the day. Azure Cosmos DB Conf 2026, in partnership with AMD, is a free virtual developer event focused on building modern, scalable applications with Azure Cosmos DB. The agenda features Microsoft engineers, product team leaders, and members of the global developer community sharing...
item.dc:creator: Jay Gordon

item.title: New Local Management Group for ALZ & Updated Sovereign Policies for SLZ
item.description: Following on from months of working alongside customers, partners, and our internal product groups, we have now made two updates to the Azure landing zone (ALZ) and the Sovereign landing zone (SLZ), that I’d like to walk you through in this post: A new dedicated ‘Local’ Management Group added...
item.dc:creator: jtracey93msft

item.title: Troubleshooting SQL MI Failover Group Creation in Hub–Spoke Network with Centralized Firewall
item.description: Overview Failover groups for Azure SQL Managed Instance provide a managed way to replicate all user databases from a primary managed instance to a secondary managed instance in another region, with stable listener endpoints that remain constant across geo-failovers. In this post, we share a field...
item.dc:creator: YogeshwaranKannaiyan

item.title: PowerToys 0.99 is here: new monitor controls, easier window management, and Dock upgrades
item.description: We’re back with a fresh PowerToys release! This month introduces Power Display for controlling your monitors from the system tray, Grab And Move for quickly moving and resizing windows, and a wave of improvements to Command Palette and the Dock, along with updates across the utility suite. You can...
item.dc:creator: Niels Laute

item.title: Deploying Azure Redis Enterprise with Geo-Replication Using Terraform
item.description: This post walks through a production‑proven pattern for running stateful services across Azure regions using Terraform. We’ll cover a primary–replica Redis architecture, regional isolation with Key Vault and networking, and a clean Terraform parameterization strategy that scales from development to...
item.dc:creator: vsakash

item.title: The Future of Agentic AI: Inside Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description: Agentic AI is rapidly moving beyond demos and chatbots toward long‑running, autonomous systems that reason, call tools, collaborate with other agents, and operate reliably in production. On April 3, 2026, Microsoft marked a major milestone with the General Availability (GA) release of Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: rajesh-yadav

item.title: Why Does Azure App Service Return HTTP 404?
item.description: When an application deployed to Azure App Service suddenly starts returning HTTP 404 – Not Found, it can be confusing —especially when: The deployment completed successfully The App Service shows as Running No obvious errors appear in the portal This behaviour is more common than it appears and is...
item.dc:creator: AmritpalSinghNaroo

item.title: Introducing AI Skill Assessment in Azure API Center
item.description: As AI skills become central to enterprise automation and intelligent workflows, ensuring their quality, safety, and discoverability at scale is a growing challenge. Today, we're excited to announce skills assessment in Azure API Center — a built-in, automated quality scoring system powered by the...
item.dc:creator: Sreekanth_Thirthala

item.title: Azure Data Studio is retired: Move your Azure SQL workflow to VS Code in 10 minutes
item.description: Azure Data Studio (ADS) retired on February 6, 2025, and support ended on February 28, 2026. The recommended path forward is Visual Studio Code with the MSSQL extension. If you used ADS daily, this guide gets you productive quickly in VS Code. The focus is on importing your existing setup,...
item.dc:creator: Iqra Shaikh

item.title: Generally Available: Bring your own AI Gateway in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Foundry Agent Service now supports bring‑your‑own‑model scenarios, allowing agents to use AI models deployed and managed outside of Foundry, including models hosted behind enterprise AI gateways such as Azure API Management. This enables organizations to integrate custom, fine‑tuned, open‑source,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Bring your own AI Gateway in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Foundry Agent Service now supports bring‑your‑own‑model scenarios, allowing agents to use AI models deployed and managed outside of Foundry, including models hosted behind enterprise AI gateways such as Azure API Management. This enables organizations toUpdate Type: GA, Services: API Management,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description: Prompt Flow will be retired from Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning on April 20, 2027 and replaced by a single, modern orchestration platform: Microsoft Agent Framework. This change unlocks powerful new capabilities, including a unified, future-proof foundation for building intelligent...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description: Prompt Flow will be retired from Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning on April 20, 2027 and replaced by a single, modern orchestration platform: Microsoft Agent Framework. This change unlocks powerful new capabilities, including a unified, future-Update Type: Deprecation, Services: Machine...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: New Local Management Group for ALZ & Updated Sovereign Policies for SLZ
item.description: Following on from months of working alongside customers, partners, and our internal product groups, we have now made two updates to the Azure landing zone (ALZ) and the Sovereign landing zone (SLZ), ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: , Categories: Management
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Local expands to sovereign-scale infrastructure with disaggregated deployments
item.description: As organizations accelerate digital transformation across datacenters, sovereign environments, and edge locations, infrastructure architectures must evolve to meet new operational and regulatory demands. The first feature update of Azure Local in CY 2026 (version 2604) marks a significant step...
item.dc:creator: troettinger

item.title: Bring your Dremio data into OneLake
item.description: Starting today, Fabric customers who use Dremio can seamlessly bring their Dremio-managed data into Microsoft OneLake — with zero data duplication — and unlock the full suite of Fabric workloads and integrations. Figure: Creating a new Mirrored Dremio catalog item in Fabric. Bringing Dremio data...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Dissecting LLM Container Cold-Start: Where the Time Actually Goes
item.description: Dissecting LLM Container Cold-Start: Where the Time Actually Goes Cold-start latency determines whether GPU clusters can scale to zero, how fast they can autoscale, and whether bursty or low-QPS workloads are economically viable. Most optimization effort targets the container pull path – faster...
item.dc:creator: robcronin

item.title: From Portal Edits to GitOps : Deploying Azure API Management Configuration with APIOps
item.description: Introduction Modern enterprise platforms are increasingly built around APIs, and Azure API Management (APIM) sits at the heart of these systems as the control plane for enforcing policies, managing access, and routing traffic. While APIM provides a powerful interface through the Azure portal, many...
item.dc:creator: aloormahesh

item.title: Rethinking Data Modeling: How GitHub Copilot Is Changing the Way We Design Systems
item.description: There is a point in every engineer’s journey where data modeling stops feeling intuitive. What starts as a clean schema becomes harder to reason about. Relationships blur. Query paths multiply. Small changes ripple across different steps. This is where data modeling reveals its real challenge: not...
item.dc:creator: B_Manasa

item.title: Azure AI Foundry vs Traditional ML Pipelines: What’s Different and Why It Matters
item.description: The Traditional ML Pipeline: What It Was Built For Traditional ML pipelines typically look like this: Data ingestion and preparation Model training (often batch‑oriented) Model validation and versioning Deployment via APIs or batch jobs Monitoring focused on accuracy and drift This approach worked...
item.dc:creator: Parvathy_R_Pillai

item.title: Azure AI Foundry vs Traditional ML Pipelines: Whats Different and Why It Matters
item.description: The Traditional ML Pipeline: What It Was Built For Traditional ML pipelines typically look like this: Data ingestion and preparation Model training (often batch‑oriented) Model validation a...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure AI Services, Microsoft Foundry, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How to Troubleshoot Azure Functions Service Bus Trigger Issues
item.description: Table of Contents Overview Understanding How the Service Bus Trigger Works Message Processing Flow Key Concepts host.json Configuration Reference Issue Categories Common Causes and Solutions 1. Connection String or Configuration Errors 2. Authentication and Authorization Failures (RBAC / SAS) 3....
item.dc:creator: vikasgupta5

item.title: Microsoft’s New In‑House AI Models (MAI‑Transcribe, MAI‑Voice, MAI‑Image)
item.description: What Are the New MAI Models? MAI‑Transcribe‑1 (Speech‑to‑Text) MAI‑Transcribe‑1 is Microsoft’s first‑generation in‑house speech recognition model. It supports 25 languages and is optimized for real‑world, noisy enterprise audio, such as meetings and call centers. Key highlights Enterprise‑grade...
item.dc:creator: kumar_rahul

item.title: Microsofts New In-House AI Models (MAI-Transcribe, MAI-Voice, MAI-Image)
item.description: What Are the New MAI Models? MAI‑Transcribe‑1 (Speech‑to‑Text) MAI‑Transcribe‑1 is Microsoft’s first‑generation in‑house speech recognition model. It supports 25 languages and is optimized for re...Update Type: Announcement, Services: , Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Dev Containers for .NET in VS Code: A Beginner‑Friendly Guide That Actually Works
item.description: What Dev Containers are really about At a high level, Dev Containers let you use a Docker container as your development environment inside VS Code. But the real idea is not “Docker for development”. The real idea is this: Move all environment complexity out of your laptop and into...
item.dc:creator: Debapriya

item.title: Azure Key Vault Auto‑Rotation Practicle Guide
item.description: If you’ve ever been called into a Severity-1 issue because a certificate expired at midnight, you already understand why credential rotation matters. Keys, secrets, and certificates sit quietly in the background and when they expire, applications go dark, trust breaks, and teams scramble. This is...
item.dc:creator: ChandraHundigam_MSFT

item.title: How ACR Artifact Cache Handles Multi-Arch Images: What Gets Cached and When Webhooks Fire
item.description: By Johnson Shi (Senior Product Manager), Toddy Mladenov (Principal PM Manager), Luis Dieguez (Principal SWE Manager), Akash Singhal (Senior Software Engineer), Kiran Challa (Senior Software Engineer), Ren Shao (Senior Software Engineer) Introduction Three of the most common questions we hear from...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: Modernizing Industrial Safety and Inspection with AI-Driven Drone Automation
item.description: In large-scale manufacturing and infrastructure environments, maintaining structural integrity is a continuous operational challenge. Industrial facilities—from automotive plants to energy and infrastructure sites—depend on thousands of structural connection points such as bolts and fasteners to...
item.dc:creator: PeterTHLee

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description: Azure MCP Server is now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb), enabling one-click installation into Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible clients. The post Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Victor Colin Amador

item.title: AI-Powered Downtime Investigation for Azure VMs: Automating Root Cause Analysis
item.description: Co-authors: Jie Su, Abhinav Dua, Mukthar Ahmed, Dhruv Joshi, Dibyendu Mondal In a previous post, we shared how Azure Automated VM Recovery works to minimize virtual machine downtime through a three-stage approach: Detection, Diagnosis, and Mitigation. This post goes one layer deeper into how our...
item.dc:creator: Jon_Andoni_Baranda

item.title: Public Preview: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description: Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN, offering a fully managed solution for backing up and restoring Elastic SAN volumes. This integration helps protect data against accidental deletions, ransomware attacks, and application updates by exporting Elastic SAN volumes to independent Managed Disk...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description: Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN, offering a fully managed solution for backing up and restoring Elastic SAN volumes. This integration helps protect data against accidental deletions, ransomware attacks, and application updates by exporting Elastic SUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Azure...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: WireGuard in‑transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters using Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). This capability enables transparent, node‑level encryption for inter‑node pod traffic using WireGuard’s modern...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available and provides VS Code tooling for Microsoft Agent Framework, including agent creation from templates or via GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry Agent Service from VS Code.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Hosted agents run in an isolated execution sandbox that provides a dedicated, secure runtime per agent session. Sessions start with a clean environment with no shared state between sessions, no cross-session data leakage, and strong compute boundaries between tenants.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Hosted agents run in an isolated execution sandbox that provides a dedicated, secure runtime per agent session. Sessions start with a clean environment with no shared state between sessions, no cross-session data leakage, and strong compute boundaries betUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.description: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available and provides VS Code tooling for Microsoft Agent Framework, including agent creation from templates or via GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to FounUpdate Type: GA, Services: Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)

This capability enables transparent, node‑level encryption for inteUpdate Type: GA, Services: Kubernetes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description: On November 10, 2026, support for .NET 8 (LTS) will end. Your apps that are hosted on App Service will continue to run, but security updates will no longer be available and we'll no longer provide customer service for .NET 8 (LTS). Required action: To avoid potential security vulnerabilities and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description: On November 10, 2026, support for .NET 8 (LTS) will end. Your apps that are hosted on App Service will continue to run, but security updates will no longer be available and we'll no longer provide customer service for .NET 8 (LTS). Required action: To avo [Sevice: App Service, Target Date: 01 Nov...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: CRC Protection
item.description: Azure Elastic SAN now supports CRC-32C checksum verification when enabled on the client side for connections to Elastic SAN volumes. You can enable CRC protection when creating an Elastic SAN or enable it on an existing Elastic SAN. Elastic SAN also offers the ability to enforce this error...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you greater flexibility to scale read‑intensive workloads. You can now create read replicas from existing ones, creating up to 30 across two levels of replication. Each source supports up to five read...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you greater flexibility to scale read‑intensive workloads. You can now create read replicas from existing ones, creating up to 30 across two levels of replicationUpdate Type: GA, Services: Database for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CRC Protection
item.description: Azure Elastic SAN now supports CRC-32C checksum verification when enabled on the client side for connections to Elastic SAN volumes. You can enable CRC protection when creating an Elastic SAN or enable it on an existing Elastic SAN. Elastic SAN also offerUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Elastic...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Designing a Medallion Framework — A Decision Guide
item.description: Everyone draws the same picture: Bronze → Silver → Gold. Three boxes, three arrows. Done. What that picture hides is the dozen design decisions you have to make inside each box — and the ones you make at the boundaries between them. Get those right and onboarding the 200th table feels like...
item.dc:creator: Subhajit1994

item.title: Enhancing Enterprise AI Deployments with Zero Trust Networking
item.description: Problem Statement Azure OpenAI is publicly accessible by default Azure OpenAI frequently asked questions | Microsoft Learn Any application with API access can call it from anywhere Violates: Enterprise security policies Zero Trust architecture Key principles of the Zero Trust network model...
item.dc:creator: kirankumar_manchiwar04

item.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise – Guidance for Azure Pipelines Customers
item.description: On March 31, 2026, malicious versions of the widely used JavaScript HTTP client library Axios were briefly published to the npm registry as part of a supply chain attack. The affected versions — 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 — included a hidden malicious dependency that executed during installation and...
item.dc:creator: Josef Sin

item.title: Fabric Influencers Spotlight April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of the Fabric Influencers Spotlight, a recurring monthly post here to shine a bright light on the places on the internet where Microsoft MVPs & Fabric Super Users are doing some amazing work on all aspects of Microsoft Fabric. The Microsoft Fabric Community team...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: When RAG Isn’t Enough: Moving from Retrieval to Relationship-Aware Systems in Enterprise AI:
item.description: The Problem In an enterprise AI scenario, the goal was to map structured feature data to relevant sections within large technical documents. At a glance, this appears to be a straightforward semantic matching problem. Initial results using semantic search were promising. However, as the system was...
item.dc:creator: ankitasarkar
